meaning: n.食,日蚀,月蚀,蒙蔽,衰落vt.引起日蚀,引起月蚀,超越,使黯然失色
examples: A total solar eclipse is due to take place some time tomorrow.
An eclipse happens when the earth and moon are in line with the sun.
Mo did not want to be ruled by anyone and it is notable that she never allowed the men in her life to eclipse her.
While Bezos does not see physical books disappearing anytime soon, he sees a certain inevitability to their eclipse.
Wrigley began marketing its Eclipse gum with the magnolia bark extract in June 2008.
